summary: Families with children entering DHS shelter roughly tripled between FY22 and FY24 (up 48 percent in FY24 alone), a surge DHS's own reporting links to asylum-seeking families arriving in the city starting in spring 2022. Entries edged down 4 percent in FY25, but the agency notes they "remain significantly higher than recent years," indicating the elevated level is a lasting shift rather than a one-year spike.
